summary.intent: Split the largest files in the Rust codebase (as identified by scripts/check-file-size.sh allowlist) into smaller modules following idiomatic Rust module organization, then verify with cargo clippy.
summary.outcome: The first batch of 6 largest files across agents, chat, gateway, providers, provider-setup, and swift-bridge crates were successfully split into domain-focused modules. The session was cut short by a rate limit reset before the remaining ~26 files could be processed.

repo: scripts/check-file-size.sh maintains an allowlist of files exceeding a line-count threshold; files under ~1,500 lines that are still allowlisted should be removed from that list
The codebase uses a multi-crate Cargo workspace under crates/; each crate gets its own module hierarchy
chat/src/lib.rs decomposed into: agent_loop, channels, memory_tools, message, models, prompt, run_with_tools, streaming, types, and a service/ submodule
agents/src/runner.rs decomposed into: non_streaming, retry, streaming, tool_result, and a tests/ submodule (basic, helpers)
gateway/src/server.rs decomposed into: helpers, hooks, init_channels, init_memory, location, prepare_core, prepared, startup
providers/src/lib.rs decomposed into: config_helpers, discovered_model, http, model_capabilities, model_catalogs, model_id, ollama, registry
provider-setup/src/lib.rs decomposed into: config_helpers, custom_providers, key_store, known_providers, oauth, ollama, service, service_tests
swift-bridge/src/lib.rs decomposed into: callbacks, chat, ffi_auth, ffi_config, ffi_core, ffi_sandbox, ffi_sessions, helpers, state, types
workflow: Parallel agent spawning per crate is an effective strategy for large-scale file splitting to avoid merge conflicts
Batching refactors by crate (one agent per crate) prevents file-level conflicts when splitting monolithic lib.rs files
10 files were already compliant (under threshold) but still in the allowlist — allowlist hygiene should be part of the cleanup pass

friction: Rate limit hit mid-task ('You've hit your limit · resets 2pm Europe/Lisbon'), halting work after only the first batch of 6 files out of ~32 that needed splitting
cargo clippy was never run — the verification step was blocked by the rate limit
open_items: Remaining ~26 files from the allowlist still need to be split into modules
10 files already under the line threshold need to be removed from the scripts/check-file-size.sh allowlist
cargo clippy has not been run — the split files need validation before the work can be considered complete
Cargo.lock was modified, suggesting dependency changes that should be reviewed
